[Wakili Notes — Whisper-large-v3 transcript of voice_note_amina.m4a]
[Recorded: 25/06/2024, 10:22, by Amina Otieno; duration 0:58]
[Source language: Swahili-English code-switch — verbatim transcript with
English gloss in square brackets where Swahili requires translation]

Habari ya asubuhi. [Good morning.] I am Amina, mama wa Brian Kamau. [Mother
of Brian Kamau.] Brian alikamatwa jana saa nane mchana, [Brian was arrested
yesterday at two in the afternoon,] na ofisa wawili — PC Kariuki na PC
Njoroge — Parliament Road. [by two officers — PC Kariuki and PC Njoroge — at
Parliament Road.] Walitumia rungu kumpiga begani. [They used a baton to
strike him on the shoulder.]

Tulienda Central Police Station. [We went to Central Police Station.] OCS
ametukatalia kuona Brian. [The OCS has refused to allow us to see Brian.]
Cpl. Otieno alituambia tuwadie. [Cpl. Otieno told us to come back.]
Advocate Wanjiku alikuja, lakini hawakumruhusu pia. [Advocate Wanjiku came,
but they did not allow her either.]

Tafadhali, tunaomba msaada. [Please, we are asking for help.] Six watoto
wamekamatwa. [Six young people have been arrested.] Hawajakuwa naattorney,
hawajaonyeshwa magistrate. [They have not had an attorney; they have not
been shown a magistrate.] Tunapendelea kuandika petition under Article 22.
[We want to file a petition under Article 22.]

Mungu atusaidie. [God help us.]

[End of transcript.]
[Whisper-large-v3 confidence: 0.93. Manual verification recommended for the
Swahili passages before this transcript is annexed to a sworn affidavit.]
